title: Charles Schwab Launches Crypto Trading for U.S. Retail Customers
summary: Charles Schwab has started offering bitcoin and ether trading to a select group of U.S. customers. This marks a significant step toward mainstream crypto adoption.
body:
Charles Schwab, one of the largest brokerage firms in the U.S., has begun rolling out crypto trading services to its retail customers. An initial group of clients can now buy and sell bitcoin and ether through the Schwab Crypto platform. This move comes as traditional financial institutions increasingly embrace digital assets.

This development is a big deal for everyday investors. It means you can now trade crypto alongside stocks and bonds in a familiar environment. Think of it like adding a new section to your favorite grocery store—suddenly, crypto is just another option on the shelf.

If you're a Charles Schwab customer, keep an eye on your account for access to the Schwab Crypto platform. While the rollout is gradual, this is a clear sign that crypto is becoming more integrated into traditional finance. If you don't have an account, now might be a good time to consider one if you're interested in crypto trading.
twitterThread:

You can now trade bitcoin and ether alongside stocks and bonds. No need for separate apps or wallets.

#Crypto #Trading

In plain English: Schwab is making crypto trading as easy as buying stocks. No more jumping between different platforms.

This is a huge step toward mainstream crypto adoption.

The part that matters: If you're already a Schwab customer, you might soon see crypto trading options in your account.

If you're not, this is a sign that traditional finance is embracing crypto big time.

Here's what you can do: Keep an eye on your Schwab account for access to the Schwab Crypto platform.

If you don't have an account, now might be a good time to consider one if you're interested in crypto trading.
